perturbation.original_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. Such services may include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc.
perturbation.changed_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. While the standard services include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc., such support is not guaranteed and is subject to resource availability.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "such support is not guaranteed and is subject to resource availability," the amendment creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement listed specific services that FASC *may* provide, implying an obligation. However, the added clause implies that even those listed services are not guaranteed, creating uncertainty about FASC's actual service obligations. This contradicts the initial implication of guaranteed support, potentially leading to disputes over the level and consistency of services provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Solely with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, the section of Exhibit A to the Services Agreement entitled "Administration and Risk Management" shall be, and hereby is, deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "2. Administration and Risk Management. Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. While the standard services include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc., such support is not guaranteed and is subject to resource availability."
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text implies a guarantee of support services from Federated Advisory Services Company (FASC). However, the addition of "such support is not guaranteed and is subject to resource availability" creates uncertainty about the actual provision of these services. This directly conflicts with the initial impression of a committed service, as the contradicted text also contains the changed text and highlights that the services listed are not guaranteed. This leads to an in-text contradiction as the agreement now both lists services FASC provides and simultaneously states those same services are not guaranteed. This is a structural flaw that creates in-text contradiction because the modified clause is in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: The change from Pennsylvania to Delaware creates a contradiction because the Adviser is a Delaware statutory trust, while FASC is a Delaware statutory trust. Changing the governing law to Delaware introduces ambiguity, as it's unclear whether Pennsylvania law (where the agreement was originally intended to be governed) or Delaware law should prevail in interpreting the contract. The choice of law is crucial for resolving disputes, and this inconsistency undermines the contract's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Delaware. This Amendment may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which will be deemed to be an original copy of this Amendment and all of which, when taken together, will be deemed to constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Pennsylvania law, but the changed text specifies Delaware law. The contradicted text further reinforces Delaware law. However, the explanation indicates that the original intent was Pennsylvania law, and now it is unclear whether Pennsylvania or Delaware law applies. This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement now has conflicting provisions regarding the governing law, which is critical for interpretation and dispute resolution. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect. The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect until further notice. The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'until further notice' introduces uncertainty. While the section states the Services Agreement remains in effect, the added clause implies it can be terminated at any time without specifying conditions. This contradicts the sense of permanence established by 'shall remain in full force and effect'. It creates an ambiguity regarding the stability and duration of the agreement, allowing for potential arbitrary termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect until further notice. The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act. This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. This Amendment may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which will be deemed to be an original copy of this Amendment and all of which, when taken together, will be deemed to constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'until further notice' in the changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the Services Agreement remaining 'in full force and effect'. This introduces an element of potential termination or amendment that wasn't explicitly present before, creating uncertainty about the agreement's stability and duration. The 'in full force and effect' clause suggests a continuous and ongoing commitment, while 'until further notice' implies that the agreement can be unilaterally altered or terminated, resulting in a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the same clause now has conflicting implications in different locations.
